Name: MegiQ
Description:
At MegiQ we create the technology that simplifies and accelerates antenna pattern measurement and wireless hardware development. Our turnkey RF tools — especially our Compact Antenna Test Range (CATR) systems — make it easy to verify and optimize antenna performance right out-of-the-box, without requiring a large anechoic chamber.

Covering ~370 MHz to 6 GHz, our solutions support most modern wireless and IoT applications. Our product lineup includes:

• Compact Antenna Measurement Systems (CATR / RMS) for fast radiation pattern and efficiency testing
• Vector Network Analyzers (VNA) for matching and component characterization
• Calibration Kits (Balanced, UFL, SMA) for precise and repeatable measurements

If you're a seasoned RF engineer, you’ll appreciate the accuracy, direct results, and no-nonsense workflow.
If you're new to RF, our educational tools make antenna and wireless testing accessible in a surprisingly short time.

The most useful tools are those you can actually afford — and our systems deliver professional-grade performance at budgets that fit real-world engineering teams.

On our website you’ll find detailed information about our CATR measurement solutions, radiation pattern workflows, and everything you need to build better wireless devices.

At the heart of Comarcom's offerings is the patented VEGA antenna series, engineered to deliver exceptional high gain up to +29 dBi across various frequency bands, including 617–960 MHz and 1710–2690 MHz and 3300-3980 MHz. These antennas feature ultra-narrow beamwidths, dual ±45° slant polarization, and options for single, dual, or triple-beam configurations, making them ideal for targeted coverage in challenging environments such as rural areas, long corridors like highways and railways, sparsely populated rural zones, remote tourist resorts, covering neighborhoods from far towers, connecting fishermen at sea and long bridges.

The VEGA antennas are designed for compatibility with all major cellular standards, including 2G, 3G, 4G LTE, and 5G. Their robust construction using lightweight, corrosion-resistant Aluminum ensures durability and ease of installation, even in harsh environmental conditions. 

Comarcom's solutions have been successfully deployed in over 75 countries, assisting mobile network operators in extending coverage, improving signal quality, and reducing infrastructure costs by up to 40%. By focusing on high-performance, cost-effective antenna systems, Comarcom continues to play a pivotal role in advancing global wireless connectivity.

Name: Enermove
Description:
The problem

The Enermove company, established in May 2019, was born from the experience of the market for electric traction batteries of the entrepreneur Flavio Cavallo, who sought a technical solution to the problem of recharging the batteries of forklifts.
The batteries used to power forklifts and trolleys, in the logistics systems, are of three types: acid lead, hermetic lead and lithium. The first ones create serious issues, since during the recharging phase they generate harmful gases and it is necessary to set up special recharging stations. The latter, despite solving this problem, necessarily require that the vehicle remain inoperative for several hours when charging. Same goes for the most modern and expensive lithium batteries.
Currently one of the most used techniques to fill up with energy consists in replacing the exhausted pack with a fully charged one. This operation is extremely delicate and requires trained personnel.
All this means waste of time and high costs.

The solution

The solution to the problem came from research conducted by Professor Paolo Guglielmi and researcher Vincenzo Cirimele of the Department of Energy of the Politecnico di Torino on wireless charging. Enermove technology consists in the creation of a charging path using coils installed under the pavement. The coils are powered to create high frequency magnetic fields, which are captured by a secondary coil installed on board the vehicle. The system allows the charge in both static and dynamic conditions. The vehicle, therefore, no longer needs to stop, then all the dangerous operations for replacing the batteries and idle time for recharging disappear, with a consequent saving of tens of thousands of euros per year. The system can be installed on any trolley and does not require the remaking of the electrical system.

WSN’s wireless sensor systems connect the physical world to the the digital world using an intelligent network platform called SensLogic. SensLogic transforms hard-wired sensors into 'smart' wireless sensors, adding a secure layer of network and analytical intelligence that converts data into critical knowledge.

WSN solutions are designed to meet the specific requirements of applications that demand high reliability, high data collection and accuracy, data analysis and reduce operating expenses.

The 2022 edition of this important event will be held in Austin, Texas. WCNC 2022 will feature a comprehensive technical program offering numerous technical sessions with papers showcasing the latest technologies, applications and services. The conference program includes workshops, tutorials, keynote talks from industrial leaders and renowned academics, panel discussions, a large exhibition, business and industrial forums.

The 2022 edition of this conference will be held in Austin, Texas, from the 10th of April to the 13th of April. You are invited to submit papers, and proposals for panels, tutorials, and workshops, in all areas of wireless communications, networks, services, and applications.

Ether’s Quick Reads of <6,000 words “fit” can easily be read in 15 minutes or less. Description:
Fundada en 2013 y con sede en Silicon Valley, FleetUp representa a una nueva generación en Plataformas para Administración Inteligente de Flotas, que a través de dispositivos IOT, proporciona una herramienta de gestión integral para el seguimiento de todo tipo de vehículos y cargas.
El liderazgo tecnológico impulsado desde Silicon Valley  y  la experiencia internacional de la compañía, avalan los resultados de nuestra solución integral que ofrece herramientas con certificación world class en la industria del transporte, automóvil  y maquinarias. 
FleetUp aplica tecnologías de big data a la gestión de flotas lo que permite a las empresas maximizar su rentabilidad, mejorar la seguridad del conductor y cumplir con las regulaciones actuales en transporte y logística de manera más efectiva. 
Actualmente cuenta con presencia en empresas que poseen flotas de todo tipo de vehículos: transportistas, buses, maquinaria, traslado de activos, entre otros.
En el caso de FleetUp Command FMS (Fleet Management Systems), se proporciona el sistema de gestión de flotas más completo del mercado, ofreciendo a las compañías de servicios y distribución una visión sin precedentes de sus operaciones. 
Esto les permite optimizar los esfuerzos de los conductores, capacitándolos desde la información entregada por lo datos procesables, maximizando ingresos y reduciendo los costos operativos. 
FleetUp Command, por su parte, se ofrece como Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) y aprovecha la potencia de FleetUp ELD, el primer dispositivo de registro electrónico aprobado por la FMCSA en EE.UU.
Evoluciona tu gestión de flotas y el control en el traslado de activos a la nueva generación de soluciones de FleetUp.

•    Avec Tech4Race, les organisateurs et les chronométreurs d’événements sportifs outdoor disposeront d’un outil pour gérer la course, savoir où se trouve chaque participant, coordonner efficacement les bénévoles et les équipes médicales.

The 6G-ICARUS project will investigate, combine, and improve on current technologies in order to address numerous obstacles that 6G networks will face in order to define the future wireless networks (FWNs). 6G-ICARUS is focused on FWNs capable of meeting a variety of targets, resulting in better geographic coverage, particularly in urban areas, higher transmission rates, lower latency, provision of diverse services, processing of information generated from a massive volume of heterogeneous sources, inherent resilience to counter potential security threats, and making intelligent decisions.

Name: Omnisense Ltd.
Description:
Omnisense, experts in making location as easy as 123, provides products and services which are leading the revolution for the adoption of location everywhere. 

Accurate positioning indoors and out can significantly improve safety and efficiency, but universal solutions remain elusive. Although conceptually simple, it is technically difficult to accurately track and positioning things in complex environments. Users and Application Developers want to know the location of things without becoming bogged down in the complexity and detail. 

Omnisense provides state-of-the-art high accuracy 3D locating and tracking solutions making it easier to manage assets - people, equipment, materials or animals - through knowledge of location, position and behaviour whether indoors or outdoors. For best accuracy indoors we support UWB based systems but for wider area and outdoor systems, at slightly lower accuracy, we support chirp (CSS) technology operating in the 2.4GHz band. We can also integrate with BLE beacon and GNSS (GPS) technologies.

We also use dead-reckoning techniques based on accelerometer, gyroscope, magnetometer and barometer, and have developed a full mesh networking stack to support device connectivity including for general telemetry and control of devices. Our core capability is in the software and systems, but we can also provide physical devices or license device designs to enable you to develop your own customised devices.

We solve the tough problem of locating and positioning things and make it easy for users and application developers to access the information they require. Founded in 2009, Omnisense is based near Cambridge, UK.

Name: WiFi Mundo
Description:
Guarantee Your WiFi Service

The problem that hotels have faced in delivering fast, secure and cost effective internet Access to guests is solved!  Hotels can now guarantee perfect WiFi coverage, network speed and guest satisfaction without the huge cost of adding infrastructure.

Using stable networking protocols, WiFiTel is the most cost efficient and high performance system for delivering guest WiFi services in a hotel. Every hotel already has the infrastructure to deliver network speeds faster than anything on copper phone lines or standard CAT5 network cable.  

Coaxial Cable Infrastructure

WiFiTel controllers send and receive data using network protocols that operate at frequencies below those used by televisions. There is enough bandwidth within standard TV coaxial cable to carry up to 320Mbps of synchronous data, OVER 3X THE CAPACITY OF NETWORK CABLE.

Within each guest room a WiFiTel Access Point exchanges data through the TV coaxial cable and delivers a WiFi connection without affecting television service.

Using the hotel’s existing coaxial system gives 4 great benefits:
1.	Using existing infrastructure cuts costs.
2.	Installation is clean and simple with zero impact on hotel décor and structure.
3.	Labour costs are minimal and installation can be done by hotel staff.
4.	Access points are low cost, allowing hotels to install one in every room.

Network Control and Support

The WiFiTel Network Management System is the ideal guest network control system, designed specifically for the hotel industry.  The system automatically heals and regulates the network, optimizing performance and user experience. Different licenses are available for Receptionist, IT Manager and General Management users. 

Financial Sense

WiFiTel systems are available under favourable leasing conditions.

We work both with WiFi Installers and Hotel Groups.

Name: Mobile Technology Association of Michigan (MTAM)
Description:
MTAM is a non-profit trade association for Michigan’s mobile / wireless industry.  We are the first state-based mobile/wireless industry trade association in the U.S., and our mission is:

-  to facilitate conversation/collaboration between varied sectors of the mobile/wireless eco-system; serve as the bridge to enable diverse organizations to understand each other's perspectives and needs in order to drive new innovation and opportunities involving these technologies
-  to increase the use of Michigan-based mobile/wireless technology products and services in-state, nationally and globally
-  to increase the productivity and profitability of every industry vertical in Michigan via the use of these technologies
-  to create sustainable jobs and increased entrepreneurial opportunities in the state based on the use of these technologies, thereby achieving substantial growth of Michigan's economy
-  to help the communities we serve via the use of these technologies

We are also the statewide producer of Mobile Monday Michigan – a mobile / wireless industry networking and education organization which is a branch of the international Mobile Monday organization based in Finland. Here in Michigan we currently have 4 chapters (Detroit, Ann Arbor, Lansing and Grand Rapids) with over 2800 members statewide.

The association's website can be found at http://GoMobileMichigan.org.

The organization is a winner of Corp! Magazine's DiSciTech award for 2013; 6 MTAM member firms also won DiSciTech awards for 2013.

Name: Moonblink Communications
Description:
Moonblink was a distributor of wireless and video surveillance equipment from 2002 to March 2016. 

Thousands of businesses around the world rely on our knowledge of wireless and video surveillance equipment. 

For over 10 years we have been doing our best here at Moonblink to provide a remarkable distribution experience. Alongside our vendors, service providers, integrators and VAR partners we have worked together to build great relationships and create loyal customers across a broad range of industries including: universities, coffee shops, manufacturers, hospitals, hotels, IT consultants, telcos, local-state and federal government agencies, restaurants, farmers, oil and gas giants, cities and municipalities, utilities, churches, casinos, law enforcement agencies and just about everyone in-between.

History: 
Like something straight out of the movie Hackers, a young Kevin Sitzes (circa 1992) was hacking into the IBM computer network at his high school and replacing everyones “welcome to IBM” text with something that landed him a hot seat in the principals office and an immediate suspension of his school computer privileges.
Meanwhile, his soon to be business partner Daniel Redmond was cutting his teeth as a top sales engineer at YDI, one of the hottest (and one of the only, at the time) outdoor wireless broadband companies around.
Ten years later, Dan and Kevin had joined forces around their common passion for wireless technology. Rather than sticking with the status quo in their lucrative jobs at the time, they decided to go out on their own and put their passion to work. They began by enlisting Kevin’s girlfriend, some of their childhood buddies, previous co-workers, and even Dan’s brother. This close group became Moonblink, one of the coolest distributors of wireless hardware in the space.

Name: GPS Networking Inc.
Description:
For over 25 years, GPS Networking, Inc. has been the Worldwide Leader in providing GPS Signal Distribution Products. We are the gold standard in providing global positioning products that allow organizations to network the signal throughout a site or facility. Our flexible product designs enable us to customize any solution to meet any GPS needs. We also offer our experience to provide clients with quality technical support and GPS network design services. We are real people who believe in building relationships not just products.

We manufacture custom GPS splitters, amplifiers and re-radiating equipment as well as attenuators, filters, combiners and Fiber Optic systems. We also offer a range of GPS L-Band, Galileo and Glonass antennas, surge protectors, adapters, connectors and custom cable assemblies. We are AS9100D:2016 certified.

GPS Networking has the experience and expertise needed to provide reliable, quality solutions for every GPS transmission and reception problem. If your company has a problem that requires a custom engineered GPS solution, we can provide the solution. GPS Networking has developed Mil-Std solutions for a wide range of industries including navigation, wireless, timing and a variety of military applications.We have provided GPS equipment for virtually every type of installation in the past, from networking GPS signals throughout entire facilities such as Motorola and Microsoft to GPS signal distribution in Bradley Fighting Vehicles.

Name: DTE Wireless
Description:
With over 12 years of experience working with the largest global companies, DTE has become the leader in the design, installation, and support of business-critical wireless networks. Whether cloud-based or an on-premise network, we offer a complete hardware vendor-neutral end-to-end solution to ensure you get the most optimised wireless network for your unique environment. 

Our experience and knowledge gained through years of creating wireless designs for all types of warehousing, from small delivery stations, to 3PL sites, to multifloored robotics fulfilment centers, has given us the ability to create designs we know will work. We will create a robust and resilient design that will work in any warehouse environment no matter what the racking type or products to be stored. 

As well as having highly trained and fully qualified wireless engineers using the latest Ekahau wireless design software, DTE also has many AutoCAD and Autodesk Revit/Navisworks engineers. 

Whilst traditional wireless companies will offer a basic “dots and spots” design for the wireless access point (WAP) locations. DTE will give you the best-in-class wireless designs utilising the latest BIM authoring software presented on professional AutoCAD and Autodesk Revit/Navisworks files detailing the exact WAP placement and mounting structure. This enables the construction and engineering companies to easily import our designs into their own drawing/model issuances, guaranteeing an accurate installation to the specified design, which also assures the WAPs will be mounted in a safe and robust way. 

Dynamic Technologies Europe is committed to the implementation and the development of BIM and have invested in state-of-the-art tools for its successful implementation. We strongly feel it adds value by creating, managing, and sharing the properties of a design throughout its lifecycle. We comply with all standards, guidelines, client policies and are compliant with BIM Level 2/COBie requirements.

Name: Power Telematics, Inc.
Description:
A generator’s primary purpose is to provide emergency power at any time. It is a critical piece of equipment that must not fail. However, it is estimated that at least 10% of generators will fail during a power outage. For many businesses and homes, power loss can threaten lives and cost money.

It is essential to know the health of your generator and that it works 100% of the time.

How confident are you that your emergency power system will work when needed?

With a Power Telematics generator monitoring system, you gain insight and knowledge on the core functions of the generator so you can rest assured that when the power goes out, you are protected. Monitoring a generator is vital to its reliability because you cannot risk equipment failure!

Have confidence in your generator with Power Telematics remote monitoring.

Name: Rewheel
Description:
4G&5G prices, competitiveness rankings, competition & mobile merger analysis, network economics and 4th MNO BC research studies, 2010–2023

Mobile data pricing, mobile operator competitiveness rankings, market modelling & analysis, competition analysis (consolidation, 4 to 3 mobile mergers, effective remedies, new market entries), MNO near-zero marginal data cost, MVNO economics, mobile capacity-only play, mobile centric convergence (MCC) pro-competitive strategies and 4th MNO business case.
Founded in 2009 and incorporated in Finland, Rewheel is a privately owned independent telecom research firm and boutique management consultancy. Our clients are mobile network operators, telco groups, MVNO groups, competition authorities, telecom sector regulators, governments, global internet firms, mobile data-centric start-ups, PE and VC investors.

Rewheel has delivered management consultancy work for clients in the United Kingdom, United States, Germany, Ireland, Switzerland, Finland, Sweden, Belgium, Greece, Poland, Slovenia, Hungary, Russia, Romania.

The following authorities have acquired access to Rewheel's research: The European Commission Directorate for Competition, the United States Department of Justice, the New York, California and many other US State Attorney General Offices, the national competition authorities of Canada, Australia, the Netherlands and Greece, the Ministry of Economic Affairs of the Netherlands, the Ministry of Industry and Trade of Czechia, the Korean Electronics and Telecommunication Research Institute, the sector regulators of the United Kingdom, Germany, France, the Netherlands, Finland, etc.

Rewheel's mobile data pricing, strategy, competition analysis, merger assessment, network economics, spectrum, profitability and competitiveness focused reports have been cited by OECD Economic Surveys, The Economist, The Financial Times, The New York Times, Reuters, Bloomberg, WSJ and publicly referenced by the UK telecoms regulator Ofcom, BIPT, etc.

Name: Xclaim Wireless
Description:
Wait, you’ve never heard of Xclaim? That’s ok, it’s a new brand. In fact, it’s a whole new way to Wi-Fi.

The Xclaim product line is built by a market leader in high-performance enterprise wireless LAN systems, Ruckus Wireless. Ruckus is known throughout the wireless industry as the leader in RF performance, building rock solid wireless systems for a plethora of markets, including hospitality, K-12 and higher education, warehousing & logistics, retail, medium enterprise, and transportation. We have some of the world’s largest service providers as our customers, and we’ve built massive public networks around the globe.

But along the way, we noticed a troubling trend. Wireless LAN vendors kept adding sophisticated features and functionality to their systems in order to satisfy the needs of the largest businesses in the world. Huge companies and massive agencies reaped the benefits of this relentless pace of development in wireless technology. All good, right?

Except in the race for bigger, faster, better networks, one thing was forgotten. You. Small business operators want great wireless. No, they need great wireless. But you don’t have massive IT staff – you may not have any IT staff. And you don’t have limitless networking budgets. So you’ve been forced, for far too long, to run to your local electronics retailer and settle for products that were designed to offer a simple wireless experience in your home.

You deserve better. That’s why we’ve developed the Xclaim product line. We’ve taken the performance, reliability and robust feature sets of big business wireless, and eliminated all of the cost and complexity. What’s left is a product line purpose built to suit the needs of small business.

We hope you find the solution simple, robust and incredibly elegant. And we hope we can help take the stress out of at least one part of your business.

Name: UNet 24
Description: UNet conference series is a forum that brings together researchers and practitioners from academia and industry to discuss recent developments in pervasive and ubiquitous networks. This conference series provides a venue to exchange ideas, discuss solutions, debate on identified challenges and share experiences among researchers and professionals. UNet aims also to promote adoption of new methodologies and to provide the participants with advanced and innovative tools able to catch the fundamental dynamics of the underlying complex interactions (e.g., game theory, Mechanism Design theory, Learning theory, SDR platforms, etc.). Papers describing original research on both theoretical and practical aspects of pervasive computing and future mobile computing (e.g., 5G, 6G, AI-driven communications, IoT, TI, etc.) are invited for submission to UNet 2024.

Name: Altus Technologie
Description:
Altus est une entreprise de Trois-Rivières spécialisée dans l'internet des objets et le 4.0 oeuvrant exclusivement dans le secteur alimentaire. (807) 698-5529

Par exemple, Altus offre la télésurveillance de la température et de l'humidité au niveau des lignes de production, des entrepôts réfrigérés et du transport. Nous travaillons normalement avec les équipes contrôle qualité, maintenance et logistique. Nous sommes les seuls au Canada dans l'alimentaire à utiliser LoRa, une technologie sans fil ultra longue portée permettant de couvrir plusieurs kilomètres avec de tout petits capteurs à batteries. Nous comptons parmi nos clients des géants de l'alimentation au Canada.

Altus est née de la passion de ses fondateurs pour la technologie et la bonne nourriture. Nous utilisons des technologies sans fils, web et 4.0 afin d’automatiser et de permettre la supervision sur tablette et mobile des différentes étapes du processus de transformation alimentaire. Notre mission est de rendre intelligente la production afin d’augmenter la qualité, réduire les pertes et les arrêts, et maximiser la production.

Dans notre vision de l’avenir, des systèmes de production plus uniformisés et intelligents permettent de minimiser les situations requérant les interventions humaines sur la ligne de production. Une personne seule peut superviser simultanément plus d’une production via le web, et ce, à des kilomètres du lieu physique de production. La chaîne de production est aussi plus flexible et s’adapte facilement et rapidement à de nouveaux produits. Résultat : les entreprises ont la possibilité de consacrer plus de temps à la création et à l’élaboration de nouveaux produits, et leurs recettes peuvent être produites à plus d’un endroit. Bien qu’il reste du travail avant que vision ne devienne réalité, chaque jour Altus travaille pour s’en approcher.

Description:

The 2-in-1 solution features:
1) a reusable tracking device with cutting-edge tracking technologies to provide precise location information; 
2) a disposable cardboard case with integrated non-rechargeable battery that powers iTraq Butterfly while the shipment travels. 

iTraq Butterfly uses WiFi, GPS/GLONASS/BeiDou satellite technology, and cellular triangulation to track and monitor shipments in the US, Canada, Mexico and Europe. Includes customizable sensors, no extra roaming fees, no connectivity charges, and 1,300 reports (expire in 1 month). Butterfly is IEC-compliant & approved for air travel.

Our unique, tiny iTraq Nano is a rechargeable hybrid (cellular+GPS+WiFi) tracking device that helps people locate loved ones, pets, valuables, and vehicles -- inside or outside, anywhere in the world.

Our customers use iTraq Nano to monitor the location of elderly family members with dementia and children with autism, both of whom are prone to wandering away from safe spaces. You'll also find Nano tucked into in our clients' luggage, laptop bags, cars, boats, guitar cases, golf bags, motorcycles, and camera bags for total peace of mind.

Name: NewAer, Inc
Description:
The team at "new air" created a software based proximity platform for Android and iPhone smartphones, enabling them to execute actions based upon the presence of other wireless devices nearby. Our platform for peer to peer knowledge enables any smartphone application to truly be smart.  Our technology runs on Windows, Android, iOS on iPhones, iPads, Apple TVs, OSX, Raspberry Pi and can leverage radio waves for automagic user interactions.

Our low-power and lightweight mobile software client (SDK) runs within a smartphone or PC, and has capabilities for a back end server (API) that can execute complex rule sets. These rules are created by developers or end-users of any mobile application software and are based upon your physical interactions with the environment.

Our showcase application Kiosk allows for quick HTML prototyping and gives inspiration to the power of our platform. Through our APIs, we allow you to develop and use our framework to trigger your own events, as an initial step before loading our SDK within your own application.

Our Share demo application allows anyone in the same space to share any file on any device instantly and automatically to the cloud.

The developer focused ProxPlatform is our core client and server side technology that lets mobile application developers on Android and iPhone/iPad iOS devices add presence events within applications. 

This technology makes smartphones truly smart by automating actions based upon people, places or things that are detected within a phone's proximity.

Winner of Best Technology for the inaugural LAUNCH event in San Francisco, February 2011.  Fierce 15 top companies to watch, summer 2013.  Seed funded strategically by Intel Capital and Deutsche Telekom in 2012.

Name: Adaura Technologies
Description:
AdauraTech develops high performance, low cost RF testing equipment that makes it affordable to test the billions of IoT and wireless devices that need to connect to wireless networks today.
 
While the wireless industry reaches new technological breakthroughs in efficiency and affordability, the supporting infrastructure of test & measurement equipment has changed very little. Previously, high cost and complexity reigned supreme. 
 
This caused problems for both small and large corporations alike:
Small startups in IoT and wireless industries cannot afford the large expense (and complexity) of yesterday’s testing equipment, which means they often don’t validate if their wireless products will actually work in the real world
Large companies need to innovate quickly to stay competitive, and in order to do so, they need high performance testing equipment that is easy to implement and use, while also being dependable enough to handle heavy testing loads for years to come. 
AdauraTech solves both of these issues. We provide the hardware for the companies that have the technical proficiency and infrastructure to perform their own testing, and soon, we will provide an all-encompassing performance testing service for companies that choose to outsource their testing and analysis.
 
Founded in 2013 by RF, microwave and system automation professionals, AdauraTech is located in California’s Silicon Valley. We currently serve customers on four continents, including Google, Intel, MIT, NASA, Telstra, Verizon and many more.

Name: Me Me Me, Inc.
Description:
MeMeMe provides the world's most advanced speech recognition platform.  Our service enables real-time voice control of any mobile application or automated phone service, and the most accurate transcription of any audio content.  

MeMeMe quickly learns how each individual user speaks and tunes the recognition process to match each person’s unique sound, delivering far greater recognition accuracy than any alternative.  MeMeMe is all about you.

The difference is dramatic.  Other systems interpret your speech by matching it against the average voice profile for all the speakers of a language, but their accuracy is low because no individual person sounds like the average.  MeMeMe’s technology matches your speech against your personal sound profile, not the average profile of all speakers, so MeMeMe’s speech recognition system is significantly more accurate than other systems.  

MeMeMe is uniquely able to deliver this dramatically greater accuracy, because we hold the patent on any speech recognition that is tuned to the individual speaker and runs over a network, including mobile, web, and enterprise networks.  MeMeMe has implemented a high-performance, low-cost, and uniquely accurate speech recognition service, available anywhere, on-demand, on any device – your plug-in voice interface for any mobile app, phone service, or audio content. 

MeMeMe can voice-enable any mobile application or networked service.  Any application on any mobile device or any phone-delivered service can quickly and easily plug us in and immediately provide accurate, reliable, and low-cost voice operation for their customers.  We also deliver transcription services for any audio content, with the same ease of implementation, and fast, accurate, and low cost results.

Name: Aer Wireless
Description:
Aer Wireless gives you truly unlimited data using ultra-fast wireless broadband solution available anywhere globally.  This is accomplished by using our proprietary technology based on IEEE standard based 802.11n, 802.11ac, 802.11ad and 60 GHz frequencies. Our networks do not require the use of towers and is not affected by the impediments that adversely affects fixed-wireless solutions, like foliage.  It’s a new technology that solves the vexing last-mile solution causing many in rural America to be unserved by the traditional carriers who use traditional technologies that are not suitable for low population densities. Aer’s networks are faster than any 4G mobile networks and in some instances 5G networks as well… with speeds of 1 Gbps symmetrically. Unlike 5G, this technology is available now and doesn’t require a new smartphone to use it, and is considerably less expensive to deploy and operate.  As a matter of fact this technology can be used to deliver the backhaul solution that would reduce the cost of delivering 5G in some rural areas. 

The unique and proprietary technology not only delivers broadband everywhere, but inherently provides the platform to deliver Smart City solutions by blanketing the deployed area with a Wi-Fi network layer that allows users to walk, run, ride and in some instances drive while staying connected to the ultra-fast network.  Our solution is ideal for:
-	IoT
-	Intelligent transportation networks
-	Autonomous driving
-	VCAS (Vehicular Collison Avoidance Systems)

Our networks elements can be rapidly deployed and enjoy the advantages of lower power consumption compared to traditional access points where Aer Wireless elements use 2-8 watts of power.  This means that we can deploy networks where others are unable to do so due to AC power requirements by using small form factor solar panels supported by trickle-charge batteries.

Name: Alvaritech Bangladesh Ltd
Description:
Our WiMAX leadership is essentially the result of our history. In 2001, when the full potential of wireless was beginning to be recognized, we took the two leading broadband wireless access vendors and merged them together to create Alvaritech. This did not just give us the means to deliver the industry's top wireless broadband solutions. It also provided us with the incredible human capital we needed to pioneer WiMAX and transform wireless communications for the future.

Alvaritech was established in 2001 with the merger of BreezeCOM and Floware, two leading broadband wireless access equipment developers offering point-to-multipoint broadband wireless access solutions. Since both companies complemented each other in terms of product offering, geographical presence, customers and distribution channels, the merger enabled Alvaritech to become a major player in the broadband wireless access market.

Alvaritech viewed the birth of the idea for WiMAX in the wake of the telecom bubble in 2002 as a new window of opportunity for broadband access. Realizing the potential of WiMAX to reduce prices and integrate better technologies, such as NLOS and OFDM, as well as increase capacity, the company became a founder member of the WiMAX Forum, the regulatory body established to smooth the way for WiMAX regulatory and certification issues.

CellWand is an award winning mobile application developer founded in 2000 with its headquarters in Toronto, Canada. 

At CellWand, our team is always striving to make it easier for people to get where they need to go, connect to the world around them and make everyone’s daily life more convenient. And now, The Ride is here. Starting in Canada, The Ride allows users to book nearby taxis, see taxi stands, plan transit options and more… all in one app. The Ride is Canada’s Get-Me-There app. Learn more at www.gettherideapp.com

Our first product, the award winning #TAXI dialing service, connects all mobile users in North America to one of the best cab companies or their preferred one by simply dialing (#8294) on any mobile. #TAXI has supported MADD Canada since 2003 and the many bar/restaurant and alcohol industry partners we work with to make getting safe rides easier. The service has been used over 13 million times to date by mobile phone users across North America. 

The Ride and Le Lift (French version) app take urban transportation to a whole new level. You can e-hail nearby cabs, get an ETA and driver details or connect to dispatch…then watch your driver arrive and rate your ride. With The Ride, you can also see transit options, plan your best route and compare time and cost for either taxi or transit.

In marketing these services, CellWand spearheads the creation of a network of marketing partners which includes wireless carriers partners, social responsibility partners (such as MADD from 2003 to 2017), big alcohol partners (such as Diageo and provincial liquor boards) and various media partners (Zoom Media, for example). These marketing partners combine to create a powerful national marketing campaign that drives awareness and usage of services like The Ride and #TAXI.

Name: Trinity Mobile Networks
Description:
Trinity Mobile Networks’ overlay software-defined networking platform, Jumpnet, makes cellular and Wi-Fi networks appear to end users as a single high-capacity, inexpensive, and ubiquitous network.  Our platform and client-side libraries (Android and iOS) are designed from the ground up for smartphones and other moving, multi-interface, and battery constrained wireless devices. We enable channel bonding, seamless handoffs, mesh connections, multi-path transmissions, and provide an Always Best Connected service. Jumpnet allows devices, network architectures, and network rules to be reconfigured on the fly. Because all the network intelligence happens on the central server, a single device can be part of infinite different virtual networks with different rules. For example, VoIP and buffered data could have different rules and routes to maximize Quality of Experience. Jumpnet coordinates cellular, Wi-Fi, mesh, and wired connections for devices running our client-side software. Jumpnet helps network operators control their network and offload as much traffic as possible to Wi-Fi hotspots.

Jumpnet virtualizes all of a device's connections and aggregates connection-states across the network. This provides network operators with a unified view of the entire network topology, including across heterogeneous networks such as cellular and Wi-Fi. The central server analyzes available connections and maintains an Always Best Connected state for each device in the network whether a device uses a Wi-Fi, cellular, or mesh connection. Network operators can choose to configure their network for maximum throughput, maximum coverage, lowest operating cost, and other optimizations. Among other benefits, Jumpnet enables automatic network reconfiguration with human-in-the-loop override and unified network monitoring. Trinity Mobile Networks also provides detailed connectivity maps and real-time diagnostics of network use, coverage, and signal strength.

.steute Technologies, Inc., through the wireless NEXY solution, is a global leader in wireless eKanban, Andon, and AGV integrated sensor networks for manufacturing, assembly and industrial workflow processes. NEXY allows customers to capture the benefits of Just-in-Time (JIT) and lean manufacturing; simultaneously avoiding overstocking and material shortages, all while keeping production lines running.

NEXY’s wireless system architecture allows for flexibility in production line design and facilitates rapid line change-over, eliminating the need for expensive wiring and re-wiring with each production change. NEXY offers a complete line of wireless sensors, position switches, reflective optical sensors, customizable multi-element command switch sets, hand controls, AGV control modules, and 3-button Andon and material requisition units. 

The NEXY system architecture utilizes interference-free wireless sensor connections that communicate using 915 MHz radio frequency (non – WiFi) signals. Real-time data integration with an existing ERP, PPS, or WMS system is available through raw XML or JSON data or via a NEXY API server. 

The company’s wireless solutions have broad applications in automotive, white goods, and other complex-assembly environments, including those utilizing automated material replenishment. NEXY solutions are quick to implement, easily scalable, and support rapid in-process optimization without interruption for OEMs, suppliers, sub-assembly, and final assembly facilities. 

Nexy is a division of .steute Technologies, Inc., providing German precision engineering in industrial wireless communication, switches and sensors for industrial and medical applications.

To learn how to transform your production and assembly environment with NEXY’s scalable wireless sensor solution, contact Rob Hargis.

Name: NYCwireless
Description:
NYCwireless is a non-profit organization that advocates and enables the growth of free, public wireless Internet access in New York City and surrounding areas.

Over the past several years, NYCwireless has built free, public wireless networks in over ten New York City parks and open spaces through partnerships with local parks organizations such as the Bryant Park Restoration Corporation and business improvement districts such as the Alliance for Downtown New York. These include networks in Bryant Park, Madison Square Park, Wagner Park, Brooklyn Bridge Park, Jackson Square Park, Union Square Park (in partnership with commercial wireless Internet provider TowerStream), Stuyvesant Cove Park (the first fully solar powered hotspot in New York), Tompkins Square Park, Bowling Green Park, City Hall Park, the South Street Seaport, the Winter Garden, the Atrium at 60 Wall Street, Stone Street, Wall Street Park, and the Vietnam Veterans Plaza, among others. In addition, the organization has created community engagement programs that take advantage of wireless networks in Manhattan, such as our annual wireless arts festival, Spectropolis (http://www.spectropolis.info).

Following the success free, public wireless Internet access in New York City parks, NYCwireless has expanded its mission to including assisting underserved communities in getting affordable Internet access. For example, NYCwireless worked with Community Access, a non-profit housing organization, to train volunteers and building residents to build and maintain wireless networks in Manhattan, Brooklyn and the Bronx. The networks will provide more than 50 residents per building with private, high-speed connections.

Die derzeit (Stand: 2018 - Quelle: Statista) etwa 1,5 Million selbstständigen Freiberufler erwirtschaften ca. 11% des deutschen Bruttoinlandsproduktes, was in etwa der Wirtschaftsleistung des deutschen Handwerks entspricht.

Name: Air-Go
Description:
Air-Go builds Location Based Solutions. Based on our proven, flexible and accurate Indoor Positioning technology we build solutions to enable tracking, guidance, analytics and hyperlocal communication strategies.
Guidance and Routing
Air-Go RTLE (Real Time Location Engine) will enable IOS and Android apps to develop indoor maps that will assist and guide visitors inside public and private indoor buildings (shopping malls, airports, museums, exhibition centers, factory plants). It provides a variety of helpful features such as indoor localization, finding destination,  shortest path, guiding and showing paths, storing locations, entering surveys for restaurants and coffee shops or finding nearest services.
Tracking
Air-Go VISIO cloud tool will capture localizations sent from smartphones and tags to display single and aggregated tracks. You will be able to manage people, assets, tags and groups to enforce alerts based on geofencing policies. Geofences are areas created in a easy and graphical way to enable policy enforcement: who/when is allowed to get in/out from a zone.
Geo-Analytics
Air-Go VISIO tool has several templates to report key geo-analytic business information such as: Dwell time, flow vectors and heatmap aggregated information. Dwell time will show you where people is spending time whilst heatmaps will inform about people density in a time period. Finally flow vectors will inform about the people and assets’s movement directions.
Hyperlocal Advertising
The combination of hyperlocal targeting and programmatic ad exchanges has opened up a whole new world for advertisers. Hyperlocal simply involves creating a zone or geofence around an indoor GPS coordinate with Air-Go Visio, and targeting all users that pass through it. This zone can be as small as a few feet in radius. Most buying platforms have either a mapping integration built-in, or allow you to upload a list of GPS coordinates that you want to target.

Name: INGECOM.ch
Description:
INGECOM is a Swiss company who has built one of the most advanced Active Tag technology available today. The products compare to the competition with better performance and lower ownership costs thanks to a totally integrated design to production methodology. The significant combination of industrial knowledge combined with the RF expertise are among the key factors of success of this company.

Founded in 2005, INGECOM is a profitable company, on the market with a significant portfolio of recognized industrial customers, like Siemens, Schneider-Electric, GE, Bouygues... Other significant customer acquisition is on the way. According to the analysts, the targeted market has an estimate 35% CAGD over the next 5 years. The sales are done through a network of partners across the world, each with specialties in the different verticals and countries.

In 2007, Siemens decided to purchase the property of the entire design of the first generation of Active-Tags, and grant to INGECOM the most advanced title of "Advanced Partner". This contract demonstrates the superior technological achievement done by INGECOM already since its inception.
The second generation of Active-Tags was born on 2008, with very significant improvement in terms of performance/cost ratio. At that time INGECOM started also to deliver products to the middle-east region and rapidly grew to become a major supplier for Arabic countries.

Today INGECOM is still growing on the grounds of its patent portfolio and highly advanced technological skills. The customer remains at the center of all the decisions of the company. One of the strategic move of the company in 2010 was to incorporate its own production site for its own products only. This production site located just nearby the R&D is a key factor of success contributing to better industrial designs with higher quality level.
This whole Swiss Made strategy, all under the same hood, is a guarantee to achieve the highest customer satisfaction.

eBrake comes to life when motion is detected, locks the device, and blocks incoming notifications. Maps, music, and emergency calling are still available as is access to all handsfree, in-vehicle infotainment systems.  

What makes eBrake unique in the marketplace is its proprietary UNLOCK TEST. It's an augmented reality based test that a driver would need to complete in order to unlock their device. eBrake is available today to fleets and insurance companies.

anunciada por Satoshisimon Bolivarmoto oficialmente el 29 de agosto del año 2015, cuando fue minado su bloque génesis. Está basada en la cultura Bitcoin, orientada a mantener el anonimato la velocidad de las transacciones y la libertad financiera. Con una emisión máxima de veinticinco millones (25.000.000) de monedas, su algoritmo de encriptación es X11 en un intervalo entre bloques de 3 minutos, esto y más hacen que Bolivarcoin sea muy atractiva para la comercialización transparente, rápida y segura. Bolivarcoin es de código abierto su diseño es público, nadie es dueño o controla Bolivarcoin y cualquiera puede participar.

Buscamos Developers Blockchain para mejoras del código, aplicaciones, test e innovaciones. Pueden contactarnos por el correo Boli.dev@mail.com o bolivarcoin@hotmail.com así como en los grupos de telegram (https://t.me/bolivarcoinoficial) o facebook de la comunidad. Incentivos en criptomonedas.
Necesitamos:

- Crear Exchange p2p BOLI/VES

Since 2016, PRIng has been designing, developing, and manufacturing innovative, customized electronic solutions for a wide range of industries.

Our focus is on safety, efficiency, and reliability, positioning us as pioneers in monitoring technology fully developed in Argentina.  

Our journey began with the creation of the first digital inclinometer for dump trailers made in Argentina, designed to enhance safety during tipping operations. This device features an inclination sensor placed on the rear axle of the trailer and a display located in the cabin. When critical inclination angles are detected, the system automatically lowers the trailer, preventing accidents and ensuring operator safety.  

Key advantages of our inclinometer:  
- Highly reliable: Certified for use by industry leaders such as YPF, Techint, and Holcim.  
- Precise measurement: Tracks lateral and longitudinal angles.  
- Active safety: Automatically lowers the trailer when limits are exceeded.  
- Advanced memory: Stores over 4 million events.  

Our products and services are trusted by a strong client base, including Bombas NIR, Hermann, Techint, and Econovo, who rely on our technological solutions to optimize and safeguard their operations.  

At PRIng, we are unwavering in our commitment to innovation and quality. We take pride in being a benchmark for electronic technology, delivering solutions that not only meet but exceed industry standards.

Name: Encore 6G
Description:
The project consortium includes the Technical University of Sofia (TUS), Technische Universität Wien (TU Wien), Silicon Austria Labs (SAL), Universidad Carlos III de Madrid (UC3M), and the Research and Development and Innovation Consortium (RDIC).

ENCORE-6G aims to establish a pan-European research excellence network in Integrated Sensing and Communications (ISAC) as a key enabling technology for 6G, by strengthening the scientific and research-management capacity of the Technical University of Sofia (TUS) through coordinated interdisciplinary research, structured training and mentoring, mobility-driven knowledge exchange, and long-term collaboration with leading EU partners, ultimately enabling joint development, benchmarking, and convergence of methods in areas such as ML-enabled RAN intelligence, RIS-enhanced ISAC, integrated sensing/localization/communications, and monostatic–bistatic–multistatic ISAC designs, while increasing international visibility, research output, and sustainable impact within the European Research Area (ERA).

Project is funded by Operational Programme: Research, Innovation and Digitalisation for Smart Transformation Programme, through the Ministry of Education and Science of the Republic of Bulgaria, Executive Agency “Programme Education”, project No. BG16RFPR002-1.009-0001 Enabling research excellence network in integrated sensing and communications for 6G (ENCORE-6G)

Name: SunWise srl
Description:
SunWise is a spin-off of University of Genoa (Italy). The idea proposed by SunWise lets the real-time monitoring of the actual energy production of a photovoltaic plant, detailing each single string of photovoltaic modules. 
The main added value, respect to existing commercial solutions, is the use of the wireless communication technology, in order to avoid the cost of cabling and to reduce the cost of installation, even for retrofitting already existing plants. Moreover, the wireless communication lets to reduce the problems related to electric isolation, and to avoid damages to the plant.
The proposed solution is composed by a set of sensing devices transmitting data from field sensors to a common data sink. Each sensing devices is equipped by modules for signals conditioning, data acquisition, and radio transmission. The acquired data includes: single string current, voltage, and the environmental data (solar radiation, environment and panels temperatures).
The monitoring activity lets to compare the expected power generation with the actual, and to exactly identify the cause (for instance loss of efficiency caused by shadowing or bad connections).

Name: IoT Futurists, The IoT Thought Leadership Board
Description:
At IoT Futurists, The Thought Leadership Board, visionaries weigh in on digital transformation technologies, including how the Internet of Things (IoT) and the ever-growing number of connected devices, things (and people) will impact and alter the way we work and the way we live. What does the future look like for industries, enterprises and consumers in an intensely connected world?

IoT Futurists move the IoT conversation forward by clearly articulating the differences and inter-relationships among Artificial Intelligence (AI), Augmented Reality (AR), Cognitive Computing (CC), Machine Learning (ML), Natural Language Processing (NLP), Robotics, Big Data Analytics and other transformational technologies. Security and privacy are, and will continue to be, front and center as more and more things connect. How are connected devices transforming education and radically changing classrooms and learning experiences? Which technologies will be job creators versus job displacers?  What will be required to keep information secure?  Whole new categories of jobs will emerge. Leading automakers are already actively developing connected car components and smart options, quickly moving toward the ultimate goal of full automation -- the driverless vehicle.

What are the economic impacts of connecting things (and people) and an increased access to data? When will these disruptive technologies become so fully embedded into all aspects of life and work, so commonplace, that the terms will disappear? What are the real drivers that will change our way of life and doing business in the future?

Digital transformation is here, and its potential to impact consumers and businesses alike is staggering. Through mentoring, annual meetings, networking and online forums, The Thought Leadership Board will educate, prepare and empower you to make smarter decisions, enlightening you and ultimately ensuring your success in both life and business.

Introducing ISAC-NEWTON: Advancing Intelligent Sensing and Communication for 6G! 

We are excited to present ISAC-NEWTON, a Horizon Europe Marie Skłodowska-Curie Doctoral Network (MSCA-DN) project (Grant ID: 101169496) focused on Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) to revolutionise perceptive mobile networks (PMNs) for 6G. By leveraging the synergy between sensing and communication, ISAC-NEWTON tackles key challenges such as interference mitigation, complex sensing environments, and high-speed target tracking. The project integrates cutting-edge metasurfaces, AI-driven signal processing, and intelligent radio resource management to enable robust, secure, and adaptive 6G networks.

Our consortium brings together leading academic institutions including Aristotle University of Thessaloniki (Coordinator), Universidad Carlos III de Madrid, Technische Universitaet Wien, Silicon Austria Labs GmbH, Technical University of Sofia, International Hellenic University, Technische Universität Berlin, and the University of Huddersfield, alongside industrial partners such as Acceligence Ltd., SmartAvatar B.V., Italtel S.p.A., GMV Aerospace and Defence S.A., Sofia Tech Park JSC, Hellenic Telecommunications Organisation S.A., Samsung R&D Institute UK, National Physical Laboratory UK, and Massive Beams GmbH.

 Learn more: https://cordis.europa.eu/project/id/101169496

Follow our page for updates on groundbreaking research, publications, and training opportunities in the future of wireless intelligence and sensing!

Name: Nikoled
Description:
¿Quieres saber cómo comenzó Nikoled? Para eso tenemos que entrar en el contexto de nuestros cofundadores, Noel de la Cruz y Eva Hernández -una pareja de jóvenes genios con muchas ganas de salir adelante en la vida, a pesar de los obstáculos-, quienes tras una serie de momentos difíciles, y de darse cuenta de todas las cosas que están mal, empezaron a buscar una forma de darse una mejor vida, y también a sus seres queridos, a su ciudad y a su país.

Cuando tienes una meta muy ambiciosa, tu modelo de negocio tiene que estar a la par de ella.

El 13 de junio de 2019, ellos -un ingeniero mecatrónico y una ingeniera en negocios- comenzaron a estructurar una empresa con una meta clara: lograr que en 5 años México llegue a ser un país de primer mundo, con Ciudad Juárez como un centro de desarrollo tecnológico reconocido a nivel mundial.

Ese fue el comienzo de Nikoled, acrónimo de Nikola (Tesla) y Led (por los diodos emisores de luz).

El 18 de junio de 2019 oficialmente arranca el proyecto de Nikoled, pero tanto Noel como Eva sabían que una meta tan grande no se iba a alcanzar solamente con dos personas, por lo que invitaron a más personas talentosas para conformar el equipo. El 22 de julio de 2019 se une David Esparza, que complementó al equipo con su talento y experiencia en los ramos financiero e industrial. El 28 de octubre llega Alejandro Torres, quien con su liderazgo nato y poder comercial ayudó a completar la primera comunidad de Nikoled.

En el camino se han unido más líderes, expertos y genios de diferentes rubros para trabajar en conjunto, ya que creemos que cada uno tiene la capacidad de desarrollarse solo, aunque eso no significa que el trabajo en equipo quede en un segundo plano.

Actualmente Nikoled está conformada por un equipo de 22 expertos, líderes y genios de diferentes rubros, comprometidos a desarrollar sus ideas para facilitarle a todos la vida con la tecnología. 

Nikoled busca ser la empresa pionera en la evolución de México.

Name: Dindog Tech
Description:
In 2016, Dindog Tech was founded as a telemedicine company with a vision to integrate technology and medical services, aiming to revolutionize the healthcare industry. We focused on developing a wireless vital signs tracking system, enabling remote monitoring and diagnosis. The system used wearable devices and advanced sensors to collect data such as heart rate, ECG, temperature, andoxygen saturation, which could be securely transmitted to healthcare professionals for analysis.
Recognizing an opportunity in the pet healthcare market, Dindog Tech created a brand in 2016 called Dinbeat, dedicated to developing and marketing the animal version of their technology. We strategically prioritized the animal market because it allowed us to test and enhance our technology in a rapidly evolving environment with real patients.This approach not only improved our system but also generated revenue for further development in the human-use sector. We gained rapid traction in the pet industry as veterinarians and pet owners recognized the benefits of remote health monitoring for animals. The system allowed veterinarians to monitor their patients' health, facilitating early detection of potential issues and prompt veterinary interventions.
To strengthen their focus and brand recognition, Dindog Tech decided to further separate the two
business lines. In 2020, we launched Plug&Health, a separated brand solely dedicated to human telemedicine. Plug&Health emphasized the seamless integration of technology and healthcare services, aiming to provide efficient and accessible medical care to individuals worldwide.Meanwhile, Dinbeat continued to thrive as a trusted brand for animal health monitoring, expanding its product range to cater to different animal species.
Both Plug&Health and Dinbeat became synonymous with innovation and quality in their respective fields.

Description:
El avisador de camareros Fast Botton, es un dispositivo inalámbrico que permite a los clientes solicitar un servicio inmediato, fácil y discreto mejorando la comunicación entre los empleados de manera rápida, económica y eficiente.

Fast Botton potenciará su negocio de una manera innovadora y efectiva, incrementando las ventas mediante la satisfacción del cliente, y reduciendo costes debido al aumento de la productividad de sus empleados.
 
El avisador de camareros, se coloca sobre la mesa y su cliente solo deberá pulsar el avisador, que se encuentra en la mesa y  la llamada se comunicará al servicio inmediatamente a través de diversos dispositivos como un reloj de pulsera y/o una pantalla LED, por medio de una señal luminosa, sonido, vibración o ambos, identificando el número de mesa y el tipo de servicio que solicite el cliente, la cuenta o un pedido adicional evitando vueltas innecesarias y gestionando el tiempo de una mejor manera, ya que podría realizar tareas adicionales en los momentos en que su servicio no sea solicitado, no se trata de desatender a los clientes, sino estar en el momento preciso en el que el cliente lo necesita.

Given all these advances we have been rebuilding the company over the last year so that we can successfully help our existing media clients and our growing stable of new brand clients take advantage of all the opportunities that this new landscape presents. Name: Monarch Antenna, Inc.
Description: Monarch Antenna has been created to commercialize its patented self-structuring antenna (SSA) technology targeting consumer wireless, industrial automation, machine-to-machine (M2M) communication and military markets. SSA improves the reliability of the wireless link by responding to changes in the RF signal environment. SSA dynamically reconfigures its electrical shape to maximize the Signal-To-Noise-Ratio (SNR). The technology is the product of a decade of R&D effort by Delphi Corporation and Michigan State University and provides an attractive business case for adoption in machine-to-machine communication, consumer electronics and military markets. SSA is a disruptive antenna technology enabling 3G and 4G devices to deliver richer content over a multitude of protocols and to offer higher mobility. In terms of end user experience, SSA has the potential to provide fewer dropped calls, smaller dead zones, faster downloads, and longer battery life. Besides staple consumer wireless devices such as cell phones, laptops, and routers, the SSA is an equally attractive solution for military applications. The technology has self-healing feature in the event of direct physical damage to the antenna structure and provides self-correction to detuning due to near-field loading through its adaptive and self-structuring capabilities.

Name: STA Safe Traffic APP
Description:
STA is an application that avoids you to be distracted by your mobile phone. At the moment you connect to your handsfree through bluetooth, the application switches off the mobile Internet connection automatically.

SafeTrafficApp aims to significantly improve road safety through the temporary interruption of the mobile Internet of drivers. As a result, drivers are not distracted by any communication that comes in on the mobile smartphone via this route while driving. The concentration of the drivers can fully concentrate on the traffic.

How.

SafeTrafficApp suspends the mobile Internet connection on your smartphone when it makes contact with the hands-free kit via Blue Tooth. Once the phone is connected to the hands-free kit the mobile Internet is interrupted immediately, thus the user can no longer receive messages from any app. Once the hands-free connection is broken, the Internet will turn on again automatically.

When.

The application ensures that the concentration of the driver remains on the road. From the time the user connects to the handsfree kit in the car, the mobile internet-connection is disabled. This proces will be registered in the logbook of the application Safe Traffic App Plus. User can choose to manually restore the mobile Internet temporarily, this is registered in the log. There is an option to connect automatically switch-off after a certain period of time if the connection to the handsfree mode is. For example, after 5, 10 or 20 minutes, at the option of the user.

Who?

Drivers are all over the world in various forms. Professional private drivers, truckers, day trippers. Anyone who uses a handsfree set combined with a smartphone would be wise to download this application, for the safety of road users, with the other passengers and other road users. Everyone in traffic benefits from this application, which inicially is free.

Get the APP: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.safetrafficapp

Name: IoT Certification Services
Description:
We are experienced professionals in wireless device certification.  Our knowledge helps our customers overcome the hurdles involved in certifying wireless devices for FCC, Industry Canada, PTCRB, CE, and more.  Two decades of experience and expertise ensures that nothing is overlooked when working with certification entities.

IoT Certification Services acts as the liaison for the following entities, to make the device certification process easier:

•	Labs – Relieve resource strains from customer issues, allowing more time for performing tests
•	Wireless Carriers – Improve customer time-to-market by minimizing process confusion and multiple cycles 
        through the certification laboratory
•	Integrators/Developers – Ensure customers understand the process and requirements throughout the 
        certification process, including education on requirements as well as reviewing test units and 
        documentation before submission

IoT Certification Services supports integrators with the following services:

•	Education on the regulatory agency and wireless carrier requirements
•	Review of all supporting documentation
•	The necessary letters and declarations that are ready for the integrator's letterhead
•	Review of the test samples prior to submission to the laboratory.  This will assure that the laboratory can 
        configure the test units in the proper way for testing
•	Review of all test reports, and when necessary, bring in design expertise to correct any non-compliance

Name: OmniMesh Technologies
Description:
What if you could cost-effectively solve 5G's challenges of density, physics, and network architecture? What if you could provide any 5G services over a lightweight, infinitely scalable platform?

With OmniMesh Technologies, you can deploy your own intelligent mesh fabric of peer-to-peer nodes, out past the data center and even past the tower edge. The OmniMesh Virtual Edge Data Center gives you the power to monetize any 5G application; to any device; over any spectrum; with any wireless or wireline resources; in any operating environment. 

The OmniMesh innovation is to bring computing resources, storage, and the flow of money down from distant cloud servers and the tower edge, and deploy them right where they are most useful: Ubiquitous and decentralized, at the extreme edge.

Name: MidWave Wireless
Description:
MidWave Wireless (formerly TerreStar Corporation) is one of the largest independent wireless spectrum license holders in the United States. MidWave’s mid-band spectrum assets cover every major domestic market, representing more than 3.3 Billion MHz-POPs of aggregate Radio Frequency (RF) resource. 

The 1.4 GHz Band is MidWave’s largest asset, comprising 64 licenses that cover 100% of the United States. At 2.7 Billion MHz-POPs, this is the largest contiguous wide-area resource not controlled by a wireless carrier. This band is unique in its nationwide footprint, attractive propagation characteristics, wide-area power authority, and protection from harmful RF interference. The 1.4 GHz Band will
soon be open to a diversity of private LTE and NR use cases in support of next-generation industrial and enterprise wireless applications.

The 1.7 GHz Band is MidWave’s indirect investment in critical AWS-3 spectrum, comprising 18 licenses that cover 19% of the nation across 11 of the largest metropolitan markets or 639 million MHz-POPs. The 3GPP standards body incorporated this portion of AWS-3 into the n70 5G band class, which was recently integrated into the Qualcomm chipset ecosystem. The 1.7 GHz Band may be used to complete existing networks using band n70 or as a generic carrier Supplementary Uplink (SUL) resource.

Name: Environetix
Description:
Environetix Technologies Corporation (Orono, ME) is developing wireless, passive sensors and systems for harsh environments based on microwave acoustic sensor technology. The lightweight and low‐profile nature of the sensor technology is achieved by using wireless microwave acoustic devices that are less than 0.5 mm thick, a few mm2 in size, and fractions of a gram in weight. Successful high temperature sensor operation results from using stable piezoelectric langasite crystals and novel, proprietary nanocomposite electrodes patented by the University of Maine and licensed to Environetix. The miniature microwave acoustic sensors can be attached to typical turbine materials such as Inconel and operate both in static and rotating parts of turbine engines, surviving g-forces in excess of 50,000 g and temperature up to 1000°C. Environetix initially targeted the development of wireless temperature sensors capable of operating in harsh environments (EVHT-100), and current product development is extending the technology to also measure pressure, strain, vibration, and corrosion. 
A key attribute of Environetix’s sensor technology is the wireless interrogation capabilities of the sensor system. Wireless interrogation enables sensor operation in harsh environments on rotating parts.

Our flagship platform, Celium, is a field-proven, long-range, low-power wireless sensor network that enables real-time monitoring without complex high-power radio infrastructure. Internationally deployed from the Southern Alps to the Galápagos, Celium integrates with a wide variety of sensors to provide timely, actionable data.
Applications span conservation, biosecurity, dam safety, civil infrastructure, transportation, energy, agriculture, and environmental management. Our solutions are trusted by government agencies, research institutions, NGOs, and Indigenous groups — from supporting the largest feral cat eradication programme on a populated island to delivery of remote area public warning systems in hydropower schemes.
We work in partnership with clients, aligning technology with local needs, cultural priorities, and long-term sustainability goals. Combining engineering expertise, collaborative delivery, and a focus on measurable outcomes, we help organisations work smarter, safer, and more efficiently.
Specialties:
• Long-range, low-power wireless sensor networks – robust in remote and harsh environments.
• IoT-enabled infrastructure and environmental monitoring – real-time data for civil assets and natural resources.
• Conservation and biodiversity monitoring – scalable wildlife management and habitat protection.
• Integrated dam safety and asset monitoring – geotechnical, hydrological, and environmental parameters.
• Design, deployment, and lifecycle support – from concept to sustained operations.

The history of Get Wireless Now goes all the way back to 1986 when two brothers, Chris and Todd Corcoran, decided to start an auto detailing business (Sunset Detailing). Chris (with a business degree from Boston University) marketed the business while Todd ran the shop. Chris soon left his job as a computer programmer to work full time.

In 1987 a customer asked Todd if he could install cell phones into his two Jaguars. A few days later and we were in the cell phone business (Sunset Wireless).

Later in 1987 another customer asked if we wanted to buy his private limousines. Why not? Now we were in the limo business (and still are, see www.sunsetlimousines.com).

Over the years we built up our wireless business, changed the name to “Get Wireless Now”, and now have locations in CT & RI with tens of thousands of satisfied customers.

Name: Personal Security & Safety Systems, Inc.
Description:
Personal Security & Safety Systems, Inc. (PSSI) Developed proprietary non-GPS location and tracking technology that employed advanced Radio Frequency Time Difference of Arrival techniques.  PSSI's R&D Engineering team was staffed with some of the brightest and talented engineers in their fields who successfully developed this first-of-a-kind system.  

The System was called "Personal 911" and its primary intended use was to immediately alert 911 police dispatchers of an emergency help request and pinpoint the exact location of where the help is needed.  This would be a crucial benefit in the event a person in a dire situation doesn't have access to cellular or terrestrial land lines, or doesn't have adequate time to make a call to 911.  

The first Personal 911 System was sold and deployed in the Town of Highland Park, Texas in 1997.  The city wide Personal 911 hardware infrastructure was completed in early 1999 and was in the final software testing stages when the Company closed.  The Highland Park System was a huge technological success and well received by Highland Park city officials and police department.     

PSSI and the Personal 911 system made national news with several prime time news stories aired on CNN and several other TV networks and major magazine articles, including Time Magazine.  

The Company closed in Mid-1999 when funding was exhausted and the Company was unable to secure an additional round of funding in time to keep the company operational.

Name: Over the Air Events Ltd
Description:
OVER THE AIR EVENTS LTD is the legal entity through which we organise the annual Over the Air  un-conference event.

What is the Event Structure?

1 Keynote Speaker, 1 Hack Day Show & Tell,  10 Lightning Talks, 12 Hack Day Award Categories, 36 hours of Hacking, 40 Workshops, and 500+ participants.

What are we Hacking?

Mobile apps, mobile web, mobile devices, prototyping platforms such as Arduino, mBed & Raspberry Pi, wearable technology, robotics, LEGO Mindstorms …everything and anything, with a focus on connected devices and “things.”

How did it all Start?

Over the Air was developed as a concept between a group of like-minded people off the back of the first highly successful Hack Days in 2007 and 2008, and the clear need in the mobile industry calendar for an event that was by developers for developers.

Instigated by Daniel Appelquist – Over the Air is the creation of Dan, Margaret Gold and Ian Forrester. Our very first event was held at Imperial College London in September 2009. Matthew Cashmore of Hack London fame joined the pioneering team at the last minute to save our hides and has been part of the core team ever since.

Ian has since moved on to be a cheerleader from afar, and a number of people have played key organising roles over the years.

Ces passerelles sont compatibles avec la technologie Longfi , une architecture qui combine le protocole sans fil LoRaWAN et la technologie Blockchain Helium. Elles sont donc aussi compatibles avec l'ensemble des capteurs LoRaWAN.

Ces passerelles fonctionnent avec une consommation d'énergie ultra-faible tout en ayant une portée de plus de 15 km en zone péri-urbaine et 2 km en ville. Elles offrent ainsi une connectivité à des dizaines de milliers de capteurs LoRaWAN avec un coût opérationnel minime.

Tout le monde peut rejoindre "The people's Network" et fournir des centaines de kilomètres carrés de couverture de réseau sans fil LoRaWAN, tout en exploitant la cryptomonnaie HNT grâce à la blockchain d'Helium.

Currently, our world is on the precipice of an unprecedented explosion in the
demand for wireless system capacity. 5G (fifth-generation) wireless is expected
to dramatically increase the number of commercial mobile base stations in the
world and decrease the size of many 5G antenna installations and their associated
equipment near the antennas, resulting from massive utilization of fiber optic
transport systems to remotely connect with network facilities. As a result, rules

The Headwater Research (HWR) portfolio is focused on inventing new technology and product designs for device-based, cloud-based and conventional network equipment-based smart services for mobile devices such as smart phones, tablets and laptops. 

HWR invented, commercialized and is now licensing the technology used in modern smartphone operating systems to control and organize network access for multiple apps running simultaneously on a mobile device. HWR also invented, commercialized and is now licensing the "Smart Service" mobile client and network infrastructure technology that has spawned a new industry market segment for providing consumers with user-friendly, on-device, instant options for purchasing and managing cost effective 3G and 4G mobile connectivity for all their electronic devices.

In 1997, Wynd expanded its technology to meet deaf and hard-of-hearing individuals’ mobile communications needs that the deaf community embraced this service, proving it highly-valuable and life changing.  Wynd was the first in the nation to provide a wireless TTY call over packet data networks and in 1998, the company shifted its strategy to focus on providing communications services exclusively for deaf and hard of hearing individuals, including a partnership with the American Automobile Association (AAA), providing emergency roadside support services. Wynd was recognized by the White House for innovation for people with disabilities and won the California Governors Technology Award for accessible communications. In 2000, after establishing itself as a market leader and innovator of communications for deaf and hard-of-hearing people, Wynd was acquired by GoAmerica, Inc (NASDAQ: GOAM), a Hackensack, NJ based provider of wireless Internet and data services that later became Purple Communications, Inc., a leading provider of communications services for people who are deaf or hard of hearing.
