Name: NxTgen Infrastructure Services LLC
Description:
NxTgen’s SaaS-based services allow cities to intelligently consider, plan and deploy commercial and publicly delivered technologies in an informed way. Service benefits extend to requirements for both publicly owned, right-of-way assets as well as private asset; essentially any service needing a municipal permit and a location in the public domain.

City Planners can take a proactive, data-driven approach to reviewing 5G and Smart/IOT requirements. while more effectively managing public assets (i.e., streetlights, traffic signal poles, etc.) as “vertical real estate” and a limited resource.

Web-enabled Reservations, Application and Permitting Workflow Management deliver detailed inventory and location data early in the process.
Reservations for carrier requirements allow planners to capture key data at initial request, prior to downstream Application Submittals and Permitting. This becomes important for 5G requirements where FCC mandates and “shot clocks” become part of the critical path. Information can then be shared across both city and carrier users. 

All parties share a view into carrier requests, the status of requirements in process, and the availability of public assets. The city can manage how and when information is displayed, with the ability to anonymize specific data. Sharing accurate, current data supports a faster, more efficient path through application review and on to construction permitting.

Municipal decision makers can visually understand what existing technologies are deployed and where future services are under consideration within city boundaries.

Name: SARACONNECTS
Description:
SARA challenges an absurdity in the current strategy to connect the world. Convention has deemed that accessing information requires hi-res display, keyboard, local processor and memory… all connected to high speed Internet. The presumed mandate for mobile broadband and smart devices has blinded most to exploration and deployment of alternative interactive solutions that might deliver more immediate benefit. 

There are more than seven billion citizens on our planet; six billion of them carry technology in the form of a cellular phone. Yet four billion still remain unconnected to the internet due to income, location, language and literacy challenges. But none of these hurdles are the limiting factor to internet access. 

When billions of handsets can't connect to the Internet, don't change the handsets...change the Internet. SARA is a browser optimized for pervasive tech – 2G and basic telephones – which means universal access to all handsets from dumb phones to smartphones. SARA delivers personalized information, content, mobile services and electronic banking to an entirely new consumer class via their current device.

In the developed world Sara delivers on-demand audio content and personalized entertainment without need for a costly monthly data plan. In developing regions Sara can be the first wireless information resource available - interactive radio where there currently is no radio or TV.

Das Beste für unsere Kunden
Zu unseren Kunden im gesamten Bundesgebiet, mit dem Schwerpunkt in Nordrhein-Westfalen, zählen namhafte Unternehmen aus dem Bereich Industrie, Energie, Transport und Verkehr sowie auch Behörden mit Organisations- und Sicherheitsaufgaben.
In den vergangenen Jahren hat sich die WFI kontinuierlich weiterentwickelt und ist heute der erfolgreiche Fachhandelspartner und Dienstleister für professionelle Betriebs- und BOS-Objektfunktechnik. Als zuverlässiger Dienstleister legen wir großen Wert auf individuelle, für unsere Kunden maßgeschneiderte Lösungen. Denn wir sind Partner auf Augenhöhe, die gemeinsam die besten Lösungen entwickeln.

Breite Kompetenz und zukunftsfähige Partnerschaften

Name: Truway LoRaWAN IoT Monitoring
Description:
Transforming Facility Operations with Intelligent IoT Monitoring and Predictive Compliance

Our IoT platform offers a next-generation solution for real-time monitoring of mission-critical environments. Designed to meet the rigorous demands of healthcare systems, maritime operations, and complex infrastructures, it leverages advanced LoRaWAN connectivity to collect and analyze environmental data—such as temperature, humidity, pressure, and vibration—at scale.

Whether you manage hospitals, ships, labs, or data centers, our platform ensures your team receives automated alerts when thresholds are exceeded, reducing manual checks and enabling rapid response. Through an intuitive portal, facilities teams can view and manage sensor data across hundreds of locations, ensuring both real-time visibility and historical compliance logs for audits and regulations.

Beyond monitoring, our system supports remote diagnostics and preventive maintenance workflows—allowing facilities managers to act before failure occurs. Our cloud-native architecture integrates seamlessly with major cloud service providers and supports end-to-end data encryption, role-based access controls, and multi-tenant management.

Key features include:

Real-time dashboards and alert systems

Sensor calibration verification and tamper detection

Predictive analytics for compliance and uptime

Role-based access controls for staff and contractors

Multi-site hierarchy and asset grouping

Integration with mobile devices and tablets

We empower operational teams to move from reactive to proactive—ensuring not just compliance, but peace of mind. Perfect for those responsible for ensuring safety, uptime, and regulatory adherence in sensitive environments.

Name: Trusted Wireless, Inc.
Description:
Trusted Wireless Inc. (TWI) is dedicated to helping organizations that rely on mission critical Wi-Fi take control of their Wi-Fi network.  

Wi-Fi is increasingly relied upon by multiple industries for mobility, process automation and handling large guest populations, but there is no design standard and it is hard to deploy at scale.  Now, the advent of next generation Wi-Fi 6, Wi-Fi 6E and 5G, complicates in-building wireless strategies.  

Trusted Wireless, Inc.’s unique service set helps organizations deploy legacy and next generation Wi-Fi side by side while planning for Wi-Fi 6E and future proofing cable infrastructure and AP density.

TWI has solutions:

•	A patented Wi-Fi DESIGN AND IMPLEMENTATION METHOD.  Invented specifically for the unique needs of healthcare, TWI’s unique design methodology gives any mission critical Wi-Fi user the ability to create an environment of pristine RF and to segregate traffic by type on multiple physical Wi-Fi networks.  Ultimately, our multi-network design helps solve the operational problems related to many legacy design methods and to position Wi-Fi as a strategic enterprise asset.  Our design team can help organizations integrate next generation Wi-Fi 6 into the enterprise while supporting legacy Wi-Fi devices throughout their useful life span.  

•	A Wi-Fi TRAFFIC ASSESSMENT service that help organizations understand how efficiently data traffic flows through the network.  Our assessment process can identify dozens of conditions that can impede the performance of a network and includes data collection, analysis, and creation of a full report of current network state, with specific remediation steps that will help improve performance.

•	WIRELESS ADVISORY AND CONSULTING service.
Contact us today to learn more about implementing a Wi-Fi platform that provides solutions for the next generation, or to conduct a Wi-Fi traffic assessment to better understand the constraints of your Wi-Fi network.  info@trustedwirelessinc.com

Name: Quasioptical Antennas Corporation
Description:
Quasioptical Antennas Corporation represents the next step in antenna design evolution for WiFi and 5G cellular communications.  Previous forms of antennas incorporate antiquated materials and design methods which were fine at lower microwave frequencies; however, future telecom will move into higher millimeter wave frequencies in order to achieve higher bandwidths and greater data streams in order to support more customers and applications requiring larger amounts of transmitted information.  Although these higher frequencies allow for greater bandwidths, they also cause materials currently in use to attenuate the signal strength which in turn lowers data rates in a counter productive manner.

Our quasioptical antenna design techniques eliminate these problems which not only maximizes signal strength reception, but increases bandwidth data rates to their full potential as well.  This makes other competing products on the market today obsolete.  Contact us today and let us help you with your project needs.

Name: Omnxys
Description:
SmartFusion intelligent antennas maintain low-latency, always-on Wi-Fi and data services anywhere. They evaluate available links and switch intelligently between satellites, frequencies, cellular and local networks to preserve connection quality and continuity, even for moving vehicles, aircraft, vessels, rail systems and equipment in remote locations.

For satellite networks, SmartFusion uses electronically steered phased-array technology with onboard control logic that acquires, tracks and hands over between satellites autonomously. All pointing, steering and signal processing are performed inside the unit across Ku and Ka bands, with multi-beam capability supporting stable operation across LEO, GEO and available ground channels.

The same control system manages links from cellular and local networks. These inputs feed into the antenna’s decision engine, which evaluates link conditions, routes traffic and moves seamlessly between satellite, cellular and local networks without interrupting service.

The SmartFusion product family includes ultra-thin antenna panels, compact terminals and embedded AiP modules. These solid-state formats are engineered for stability under vibration, motion, temperature variation and weather exposure. The hardware is built on a complete chip-to-product design chain, integrating RF chips, beam-forming ICs and AiP modules into finished systems for commercial, industrial and operational environments.

Learn more: https://omnxys.com

Name: Böle
Description:
Right now, around 71% of the Earth's surface is covered with water. Only 2.5% of that is fresh water. And 0,62% of that is potentially consumable.

This means that of all the water that is exposed on Earth’s surface, only 0,44% is potentially consumable, and that tiny portion is exposed to pollution clusters that jeopardize the integrity of those water sources. Form poor or nonexistent sewage treatment to lackluster urban planning, the consumption of clean water is not guaranteed for the vast majority of world population. Only 16% of the world's nations fulfill basic sanitation requirements for their population, and 3,2 million deaths can be attributed to unsanitary water consumption every year. This is even without considering the impact of this same issue in other species and whole ecosystems
So, we at Böle decided to tackle this issue with crowd testing water sources in an immediate and reliable way. There are a lot of researchers out there that can work with this, but there are even more regular people that can be benefited and will be interested in diagnosing water sources near them, and that is where the solution lies. To do this we developed an app and are developing a companion device that can take measurments of vital information form a water soruce, just using a couple drops of it. Things like pH, e coli, clorine levels and turbidity can be measured by this device and be immediately reported to the app and uploaded to make a global profile of the potentially usable water sources. This information will then be made available to governments, industries and individuals through a site, and we can make projections of ways to decrease water stress from already used water sources, better water sourcing distribution and water source recovery.

Description:
Save Cell was developed with the belief that all Canadians (including ourselves) were paying too much for communications services. Between our cell phones, cable TV, internet and landlines, the costs were often more than we expected and more importantly, wanted to spend. We knew we were not alone and after doing some market research, we found that countless Canadians felt the same way, both in their business and personal lives. We knew there had to be a way to use these services without breaking the bank. And we were 100% committed to finding the solution.

We believe that all large corporations, small to medium sized businesses, families and individuals alike should be able to use these communications services without having to spend more than is necessary. We studied the industry, learned the ins and outs, made contacts within all the providers and promised our clients we would find ways to help reduce their spend moving forward. We assured our clients we were never going to switch their provider or their service. Our goal was simply to get them better pricing for the services they were already paying for. We wanted to save them both time and money.

We believe there is no better way to put your money where your mouth is than to offer our analysis 100% free of charge. We are that confident in our ability to reduce our clients’ communications spend that we are willing to perform the analysis for free just to see what we can save for you. If we can’t save you money than we don’t deserve to be paid. But we know that we can and we offer a risk free solution for doing so. In a time where value is hard to come by, you wont find better value than this with our contingency based pricing. To date we have saved our clients over $6,000,000.00 and this number continues to grow hand in hand with our company.

maison connectée

La maison sait reconnaître votre présence et peut activer ou désactiver l’alarme en conséquence. Name: Tekview Limited
Description:
Machine to machine (M2M) GSM products for remote power control and power loss monitoring/alerting.  

We have been providing GSM (mobile) remote access power products to large corporates and SMEs for over 4 years and have completed some large rollouts in this time, such as the Amazon smart lockers.  

We have developed a brand new product for 2015, Powertxt Duo, which allows remote power control of two independent devices over the GSM network (global system for mobile communication) reducing downtime of your equipment to a minimum.

Name: Mee
Description:
Mee ir inovatīvs risinājums, neatņemamai biznesa sastāvdaļai. Katrs no mums savā darba ikdienā daloties ar kontaktiem izmanto vizītkarti. 
Kāda parasti ir vizītkarte - neliels 9*5cm papīra gabaliņš ar Jūsu pamata kontaktiem, kuru nododat tālāk cilvēkam ar ko vēlaties dibināt tālākus kontaktus.
Ko Jūs teiktu, ja tam būtu kāda alternatīva, kura nekad neizsītks, kas ļauj nodot daudz vairāk informācijas vienā reizē, neapgrūtinot informācijas saņēmēju?
Tieši šadu risinājumu Jums piedāvā Mee. Mee ir mūsdienīgs bezkontakta vizītkartes risinājums, kas ļauj ar vienu bii nodot savus kontaktus otram, nekādā veidā nekaitējot dabai, iespējams nodot daudz lielāku kontaktu apjomu un tas nemaz nav sarežģīti.

Izmantojot bezkontakta tehnoloģijas, kuras šobrīd pieejamas visās jaunākajās mobilajās ierīcēs, Mee vizītkarte glabā visu Jūsu norādīto kontaktinformāciju. Tikai pieliekot savu Mee bezkontakta vizītkarti pie otra cilvēka mobilās ierīces, tajā uzreiz tiek nodoti Jūsu kontakti. 
Vai aizdomājāties kā tas atvieglo un uzlabo Jūsu darba ikdienu?
1. Nav jādomā par to, ka vizītkartes ir beigušās, jo Mee ir pēdējā vizītkārte, kuru Jums būs nepieciešams iegādāties.
2. Mainoties Jūsu kontaktiem, tos iespējams izmainīt savā kontaktu profilā, nav nepieciešams drukāt jaunas vizītkartes.
3. Jūs nekaitējat dabai, jo viens pīkstiens kāda mobilajā telefonā aizstās vienu lieki iztērētu papīra vizītkārti.
4. Negadīsies neērtās situācijas, kad uz tišanos nav paņemtas vizītkartes, jo līdzīgi kā bankas karte, Mee vizītkarte vienmēr būs Jūsu makā, tā nevienam nav jānodod un tā vienmēr ir ar Jums.
Šādus plusus var pieminēt vēl un vēl, tomēr galvenā doma visā tajā jau ir skaidra. 
Izmantojiet jauno Mee bezkontakta vizītkaršu risinājumu, saudzējiet davu un atvieglojiet savu ikdienu.

Name: GenieView Inc.
Description:
GenieView is the world leader and pioneer in real-time non-line-of-sight video streaming solutions. Wireless transmission based live video streaming has many advantages, including mobility, fast deployment, and flexible range. However, analog and optical radio technology, when used for wireless transport, have one major deficiency - they need line-of-sight (LOS) conditions for the video streaming to happen.

GenieView’s patented technology enables video streaming through barriers, including building walls and steel doors. Currently, company's products: GV1500, GV2500 and GV3500, that use a standard 900 MHz HF digital or GSM radio, achieve non-line-of-sight (NLOS) wireless streaming over a long operating range, with H.265 equivalent bit efficiency. This makes them an ideal choice for mine industry, online fast delivery service, environmental watch, first response personnel, high-end delivery vehicle requiring the real-time capture and transmission of video information over a long distance.

Name: Wireless Internet Institute
Description:
The Wireless Internet Institute (W2i) produces superior programs and editorial content addressing the need to explore, think and learn in an interactive atmosphere highly conducive to establishing relationships, alliances and partnerships.

W2i began its thought-leadership activities with a retreat, The New Dynamics of the Wireless Internet, at the Georgian Terrace Hotel in Atlanta in the fall of 2002. Immediately afterward, W2i formed the Spectrum Policy Group to explore the Federal Communication Commission’s landmark reform of its entire spectrum policy.

On June 26, 2003, in conjunction with the United Nations Information and Communication Technology Task Force (UNICTTF), W2i hosted The Wireless Internet Opportunity for Developing Countries at UN Headquarters in New York, creating the conditions for informal dialogue and brainstorming among industry practitioners, government representatives and international development experts.

In November 2003, W2i hosted the first Wireless Internet and Municipal Government Summit in cooperation with the Office of the Mayor in Atlanta. The findings were submitted as a report to the first World Summit of Cities and Local Authorities in Lyon, France later that December.

In 2003 and 2005, W2i was a delegate to the World Summit on the Information Society (WSIS). In preparation for Geneva 2003, W2i, the UN ICT Task Force and the infoDev program of the World Bank co-published The Wireless Internet Opportunity for Developing Countries.

In spring of 2004, within the WSIS framework, W2i launched the Global Municipal Government and Local Authorities Series with the UN Information and Communications Technologies Task Force, including programs in Amman, Jordan; San Mateo, California (report); Monterrey, Mexico; and Rio de Janeiro.

In preparation for the World Summit on the Information Society in Tunis 2005, W2i and the UN ICT Task Force co-published The Promise of Broadband Wireless Communities thanks to a grant from Intel Corp.

In spring of 2005, W2i prepared the Intel white paper Digital Communities Best Practices, and in May 2005, W2i co-hosted the inaugural Digital Cities Convention with the leadership of the City of Philadelphia. Attracting 200-300 local-government decisionmakers and IT professionals, and the ecosystem of broadband-wireless equipment, service, and application providers and systems integrators, the Convention has traveled around the world since 2005 to the following host cities: Philadelphia (2X), Shanghai, San Francisco, Bilbão, Houston, Rio de Janeiro, Los Angeles, Taipei, London (2X), Phoenix, hicago and Washington DC.

W2i has launched in 2008 a new initiative, the Wireless Governement Report, and is presently preparing a new program of shared information and webinars specifically addressed to video monitoring wireless systems, MetroVideoMonitor.

TrackMe es un sistema para encontrar personas, animales y objetos extraviados al instante desde una aplicación instalada en un teléfono móvil, tablet o, incluso, desde un ordenador. Se trata de aprovechar las nuevas formas de localización gracias al GPS y los sistemas de envío de ubicación desde los dispositivos móviles para rastrear aquello o aquél que se nos ha perdido.

TrackMe somos un equipo que cree que los avances tecnológicos deben servir para hacer la vida más fácil a las personas. Por eso, queremos acercar a los usuarios las ventajas que la comunicación sin cables y los sistemas de satélite pueden traer a su vida diaria. Y, en esta vida diaria, puede ocurrir que se pierda un niño, se desoriente un anciano a nuestro cuidado o nuestra mascota haya decidido aventurarse lejos y no sepa regresar a su hogar. Del mismo modo, cualquiera puede ser víctima de un robo de su vehículo. Todas estas cosas pasan muy a menudo (las estadísticas así lo atestiguan) y TrackMe es una solución fácil, inmediata y asequible a todos los bolsillos.

Name: GoAmerica, Inc.
Description: Founded in 1996 by Aaron Dobrinsky and Joe Korb, GoAmerica was truly a pioneer in the wireless Internet services industry.  The companyâs flagship service was a breakthrough web browsing application called Go.Web.  The award winning Go.Web application was designed to optimize web browsing on BlackBerry and Palm OS devices and laptops; all of which relied on narrowband wireless data networks.   GoAmerica assembled an impressive partner roster for its Go.Web services which included Dell, IBM, Research in Motion, Microsoft, Rogers Cantel, Hewlett Packard, Compaq, and Earthlink among others.   In April 2000, GoAmerica acquired a wireless data services company called Wynd Communications which had been designing its services for people who are deaf or hard of hearing.  In 2003 GoAmerica shifted its strategy to serving the deaf community entirely and began to introduce new Internet-based text and video relay services and five years later acquired a business unit from Verizon and merged with HandsOn Video Relay, a California based provider of broadband video relay services for the deaf.  The resulting company today operates as Purple Communications, Inc.  Purple is a market leader and continues to serve people who are deaf or hard of hearing with the same innovation and creative spirit by which the company was founded.

Name: WirelessWall LLC
Description:
WirelessWall is a best-of-breed, proven solution for securing wireless local area networks (WLANs) used in some of the most sensitive and demanding environments. It is the industry’s premier FIPS 140-2 validated software-based solution for protecting wireless networks at the Layer 2 level and Mesh-certified software solution. This security solution overlays RAIbotics Artificial Intelligence/Robotics Products.

We're a green solution that saves you time and money. Our VMware appliances even let you run controllers under Windows, Linux or Macs you already own to give you across-the-board strong security and privacy everywhere, instantly, with zero capital equipment investment. WirelessWall provides Blanket Security
WirelessWall:
-	Enforces uniform high (WPA2-grade) security across the board, regardless of access point (AP) vendor. Because it is independent of the radio technology being used, it can support any mix of 802.11a, b, g, j or 802.11n access points from any vendor, and can operate across long-haul bridges such as 802.16 (WiMax).
-	Protects existing infrastructure investment by enabling strong security on legacy APs and client WLAN cards that may only support WEP or WPA.
-	Centralizes firewall and port-management policies for large clusters of access points, providing a simplified, common management interface that would otherwise have to be replicated to each access point.  It allows for more rapid response to security changes, and reduces TCO for administering large clusters of APs.
-	Offers virtualization and cloaking to provide full mobility to allow users to roam between APs and stay securely connected. This architecture also supports failover and redundancy.
-	Provides true end-to-end security by encrypting the network from every client all the way to the datacenter. WirelessWall closes the security gap, securing the datacenter to each wireless laptop, desktop or mobile device

Name: Xondas, Inc.
Description:
Founded by researchers at the University of Michigan, Xondas Inc. is dedicated to advancing the state of the art in wireless power transfer.  Xondas provides engineering consulting and conducts technology development to improve wireless power transfer products based on resonant magnetic induction.

Xondas can significantly improve the safety and efficiency of resonant wireless power transfer systems by designing sophisticated emitters using our patented near-field technology, which allows for the tailoring of the radio-frequency magnetic field to an unprecedented degree.  Such tailoring allows for higher efficiency, yielding less device heating, less RF exposure, and the ability to use smaller coils at a given charging distance.  In addition, the near-field technology can suppress fringe magnetic fields in unwanted directions, without heavy shielding, for situations where RF exposure or EMI is a concern.  Our near-field plates can be manufactured inexpensively using printed circuit board technology and off-the-shelf capacitors, and do not limit the power level or operating frequency of the overall system.

Name: DOMOZIM
Description:
Grâce à des capteurs intégrés au coeur de la maison, celle-ci devient communicante.

Cela permet de connaître son état à tout moment :

les lumières qui sont allumées
la température dans chaque pièce
l’état des ouvertures (portes, fenêtres…)
les pièces occupées
…
A partir de ces états, il est possible d’agir sur des modules de commande :

marche/arrêt d’une lumière
marche/arrêt du chauffage
marche/arrêt d’un appareil électrique
…
Chaque action pourra aussi être contrôlée de manière automatique, par exemple à horaire fixe, ou avec des modules de commande (télécommandes, écrans tactiles…)

La domotique permet avant tout d’améliorer la vie au quotidien en facilitant certaines tâches, et en intégrant les technologies d’aujourd’hui directement au centre de la maison.

Film de présentation de la domotique
Voici un petit film qui vous présente les applications courantes de la domotique.


La domotique, comment ça marche ?
Il existe plusieurs technologies utilisées en domotique.

Le principe de chacune d’entre elle est de faire communiquer les équipements entre eux.

Par exemple, un capteur indique que la fenêtre du salon est ouverte. Le radiateur du salon est alors automatiquement coupé au bout de 30 secondes pour éviter de chauffer inutilement.

Parmi toutes ces technologies, nous pouvons distinguer trois grands types :

Technologie par courant porteur (cette technologie utilise le réseau EDF existant, et permet ainsi de faire le minimum de travaux dans la maison.)
Technologie par onde radio (les éléments communiquent entre eux par ondes radios: wifi, bluetooth, 433Mhz…)
Technologie câblée propriétaire (bus CAN, bus 1-wire…)
Vous pouvez retrouver sur notre site ces différentes technologies, par exemple Legrand utilise les technologies CPL et sans fils, Chacon utilise le sans fils…

Name: SpriteGuard - Protecting open access WiFi
Description:
SpriteGuard is a software and hardware hybrid solution developed by TOM Limited. The software is deployed within an access point, user activity is then monitored and reported on via a cloud based management console, thus offering data rich analytics for user activity as well as a wholly unique security solution.

The system sits outside of, and runs separately to the existing WiFi network, as a result SpriteGuard observes and protects the whole of the wireless environment, not just devices connected to the primary network.

SpriteGuard can be effectively and efficiently deployed without the need to replace any legacy access points offering a seamless security solution at a “game changer” level. SpriteGuard is, without a doubt, a completely unique offering within the Software as Security market.

Currently, most providers of free public WiFi access are confident that those who connect to access points that are not provided by them are not their concern or issue. This is, of course, a false assumption and can be extremely damaging to user confidence and brand reputation if a breach is incurred by a client logging onto a rogue site in error. 

SpriteGuard is squarely aimed at brands enhancing their reputation by offering a total security solution to any client who uses free WiFi access whilst in their facility.

Where can I use CLEAR?

Name: DOMYTECK
Description:
Grâce à des capteurs intégrés au coeur de la maison, celle-ci devient communicante.

Cela permet de connaître son état à tout moment :

les lumières qui sont allumées
la température dans chaque pièce
l’état des ouvertures (portes, fenêtres…)
les pièces occupées
…
A partir de ces états, il est possible d’agir sur des modules de commande :

marche/arrêt d’une lumière
marche/arrêt du chauffage
marche/arrêt d’un appareil électrique
…
Chaque action pourra aussi être contrôlée de manière automatique, par exemple à horaire fixe, ou avec des modules de commande (télécommandes, écrans tactiles…)

La domotique permet avant tout d’améliorer la vie au quotidien en facilitant certaines tâches, et en intégrant les technologies d’aujourd’hui directement au centre de la maison.

Film de présentation de la domotique
Voici un petit film qui vous présente les applications courantes de la domotique.


La domotique, comment ça marche ?
Il existe plusieurs technologies utilisées en domotique.

Le principe de chacune d’entre elle est de faire communiquer les équipements entre eux.

Par exemple, un capteur indique que la fenêtre du salon est ouverte. Le radiateur du salon est alors automatiquement coupé au bout de 30 secondes pour éviter de chauffer inutilement.

Parmi toutes ces technologies, nous pouvons distinguer trois grands types :

Technologie par courant porteur (cette technologie utilise le réseau EDF existant, et permet ainsi de faire le minimum de travaux dans la maison.)
Technologie par onde radio (les éléments communiquent entre eux par ondes radios: wifi, bluetooth, 433Mhz…)
Technologie câblée propriétaire (bus CAN, bus 1-wire…)
Vous pouvez retrouver sur notre site ces différentes technologies, par exemple Legrand utilise les technologies CPL et sans fils, Chacon utilise le sans fils…

Name: ICEcare
Description:
Big brands require a new level of customer loyalty and corporate image; otherwise they are going to find themselves in a constant cycle of lowering prices to stave off so many new competitors and emerging business models. Retailers need to connect with their customers and communities on an emotional and personal level. Great marketing has always elicited emotions and what better way to elicit emotions than telling your customers you care about them and their family’s safety and well being. Mobile phones are the most personal consumer devices ever invented, so why is the current mobile marketing limited to SMS and mobile optimized websites?  Why are so many retailers, enterprises and NPO’s laggards in developing and deploying mobile applications and services?  A major reason; they don’t know what to offer their customers other than an optimized-for-mobile website; or a digital version of their weekly snail-mail flyer.  In addition they do not have the talent base in-house to develop and deploy advanced mobile services.  There is huge pent-up demand from major brands for a turnkey mobile solution that allows enterprises to offer wireless subscribers mobile applications quickly with built-in direct marketing options.  ICEcare allows full rich text and HTML5 messages to be sent to your customers that will open new sales and marketing channels to build brands, increase sales, acquire new customers, improve loyalty, reduce churn, and increase corporate citizenship and philanthropy. 

ICEcare is a comprehensive emergency preparedness solution for mobile devices that provides you and your family the tools required to prepare for, survive and recover from an emergency or disaster.  With ICEcare your phone will contain medical information and emergency contacts so first responders and hospital staff can provide informed-care in the event you are rendered incoherent or unconscious due to a serious accident or medical emergency; even if the phone is inoperable or locked.
